does: This paper investigates the role of individual attention heads in maintaining safety capabilities of large language models. The authors introduce Safety Head ImPortant Score (Ships), a novel metric designed to quantify each attention head's contribution to model safety, and develop Safety Attention Head AttRibution Algorithm (Sahara) to identify which attention heads are critical for safety functionality at both the model and dataset levels. Through mechanistic analysis, they demonstrate that suppressing or disabling specific identified safety-critical attention heads compromises the model's ability to refuse harmful requests, revealing that safety is not uniformly distributed across the attention mechanism but concentrated in particular heads.
connects: This work builds on recent mechanistic interpretability research examining how safety mechanisms are implemented within neural networks, extending earlier findings that safety representations can be isolated and studied. It responds to a gap in existing safety research that has focused on identifying safety components and representations but largely ignored the multi-head attention architecture despite its fundamental role in LLM functionality. The paper combines insights from attention analysis literature with safety-focused interpretability work, drawing on techniques for head attribution and component importance scoring that have been developed for understanding other model behaviors.
enables: This research enables targeted interventions for improving LLM safety by identifying which specific attention heads require protection or strengthening. It opens pathways for developing more robust safety mechanisms by understanding how safety emerges from distributed architectural components. Future work could leverage these findings to design adversarial defenses that specifically protect critical safety heads, develop interpretable safety verification methods, or create more efficient fine-tuning approaches that preserve safety-critical components. The methodology could also inform architectural designs for safer LLMs that more explicitly incorporate safety-related attention patterns.
